web_text: Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes, to the assembly of God which is at Corinth; those who are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, with all who call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place, both theirs and ours: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. I always thank my God concerning you, for the grace of God which was given you in Christ Jesus;
slang_text: This is Paul here, totally called out to be an apostle of Jesus Christ 'cause that's what God wanted, and our bro Sosthenes is tagging along too. Writing to God's crew in Corinth -- you guys who got made holy in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, right along with everybody everywhere who calls on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, their Lord and ours. Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. I always thank my God about you guys, for the totally righteous grace God gave you in Christ Jesus;

web_text: that in everything you were enriched in him, in all speech and all knowledge; even as the testimony of Christ was confirmed in you: so that you come behind in no gift; waiting for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ; who will also confirm you until the end, blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
slang_text: 'cause in every single way you got totally loaded up in him, with mad skills in talking and in knowing stuff -- the word about Christ really stuck in you solid. So you're not missing out on a single gift, dudes, just chilling and waiting for our Lord Jesus Christ to show up again. And he's gonna keep you solid all the way to the end, squeaky clean on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.

web_text: God is faithful, through whom you were called into the fellowship of his Son, Jesus Christ, our Lord. Now I beg you, brothers, through the name of our Lord, Jesus Christ, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be perfected together in the same mind and in the same judgment. For it has been reported to me concerning you, my brothers, by those who are from Chloe’s household, that there are contentions among you. Now I mean this, that each one of you says, “I follow Paul,” “I follow Apollos,” “I follow Cephas,” and, “I follow Christ.”
slang_text: God's for real faithful, and he's the one who called you into hanging out with his Son, Jesus Christ, our Lord. Now I'm begging you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, get on the same page and quit with the bogus divisions among you. Get it together, same mind, same game plan. 'Cause word got back to me, my brothers, from Chloe's household people, that y'all are straight up beefing with each other. Here's what I mean -- one of you says "I'm with Paul," another says "I'm with Apollos," another says "I'm with Cephas," and another says "I'm with Christ." Talk about trippin'.

web_text: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paul? I thank God that I baptized none of you, except Crispus and Gaius, so that no one should say that I had baptized you into my own name. (I also baptized the household of Stephanas; besides them, I don’t know whether I baptized any other.)
slang_text: Is Christ split up into pieces or something? Was Paul the one who got nailed to a cross for you? Did you get baptized in Paul's name? No way. I thank God I didn't baptize a single one of you, 'cept Crispus and Gaius, so nobody can say I baptized you into my own name like I'm some kind of big deal. (Oh yeah, I also baptized Stephanas's household -- other than that, I honestly can't remember baptizing anybody else.)

web_text:
For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the Good News—not in wisdom of words, so that the cross of Christ wouldn’t be made void. For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are dying, but to us who are saved it is the power of God. For it is written,
“I will destroy the wisdom of the wise,
I will bring the discernment of the discerning to nothing.” Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the lawyer of this world? Hasn’t God made foolish the wisdom of this world?
slang_text:
'Cause here's the deal -- Christ didn't send me out to baptize people, he sent me to preach the Good News, and not with fancy smart-sounding words either, so the cross of Christ doesn't get watered down into nothing. The message about the cross sounds totally bogus and foolish to people who are headed for the dumpster, but to us who are getting saved, it's straight-up God's power. 'Cause it's written:
"I'm gonna wreck the wisdom of the wise,
I'm gonna toss the smarts of the smart guys right in the trash."
So where's the wise dude now? Where's the scribe? Where's this world's fancy lawyer? Hasn't God made this whole world's wisdom look totally bogus?

web_text: For seeing that in the wisdom of God, the world through its wisdom didn’t know God, it was God’s good pleasure through the foolishness of the preaching to save those who believe. For Jews ask for signs, Greeks seek after wisdom, but we preach Christ crucified; a stumbling block to Jews, and foolishness to Greeks, but to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God.
slang_text: See, in God's wisdom, the world with all its smarts totally missed knowing God. So God decided it was choice to use this so-called foolish preaching thing to save whoever believes. Jews want signs, Greeks are out there chasing wisdom, but we just preach Christ getting crucified -- a stumbling block to the Jews and complete foolishness to the Greeks. But to the ones who are called, Jews and Greeks alike, Christ is God's power and God's wisdom, no duh.

web_text: Because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men. For you see your calling, brothers, that not many are wise according to the flesh, not many mighty, and not many noble; but God chose the foolish things of the world that he might put to shame those who are wise. God chose the weak things of the world, that he might put to shame the things that are strong; and God chose the lowly things of the world, and the things that are despised, and the things that are not, that he might bring to nothing the things that are:
slang_text: 'Cause even God's so-called foolishness is way smarter than any human, and God's so-called weakness is way stronger than any human. Check out who got called, brothers -- not a ton of wise-guy geniuses, not many bigwigs, not many blue bloods. Nope, God picked the foolish stuff of the world to totally embarrass the so-called wise. God picked the weak stuff of the world to embarrass the strong. God picked the lowly stuff, the stuff everybody looks down on, the stuff that's basically nothing, just to wipe out the stuff that thinks it's something:

web_text: that no flesh should boast before God. Because of him, you are in Christ Jesus, who was made to us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification, and redemption: that, according as it is written, “He who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.”
slang_text: so that no human ever gets to brag in front of God like a boss. It's 'cause of him that you're in Christ Jesus, who became our wisdom straight from God, and our righteousness, and our sanctification, and our redemption. So like it's written, "Whoever's gonna brag, let him brag on the Lord" -- and that's the only bragging that's legit.

web_text: When I came to you, brothers, I didn’t come with excellence of speech or of wisdom, proclaiming to you the testimony of God. For I determined not to know anything among you, except Jesus Christ, and him crucified. I was with you in weakness, in fear, and in much trembling. My speech and my preaching were not in persuasive words of human wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power,
slang_text: Look, when I first rolled up on you guys, I wasn't out there tryin' to sound like some smooth-talkin' egghead droppin' fancy words to impress everybody -- I just laid down the straight-up truth about God, no frills. I made up my mind I wasn't gonna know squat except Jesus Christ, and Him nailed to that cross, period. Honestly? I showed up shaky, kinda freaked, sweatin' bullets the whole time. My preachin' wasn't some slick sales pitch built on clever human words -- nah, it was the real deal, backed up by the Spirit showin' off some serious power.

web_text: that your faith wouldn’t stand in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God. We speak wisdom, however, among those who are full grown; yet a wisdom not of this world, nor of the rulers of this world, who are coming to nothing. But we speak God’s wisdom in a mystery, the wisdom that has been hidden, which God foreordained before the worlds for our glory, which none of the rulers of this world has known. For had they known it, they wouldn’t have crucified the Lord of glory.
slang_text: So your faith wouldn't be propped up on some dude's clever brainpower, but on God's raw power instead. Now, don't get me wrong -- we do kick it with some righteous wisdom, but only around folks who are grown-up in the faith, not spazzin' out. And it ain't the wisdom of this world, or of the big-shot rulers runnin' the show -- those cats are totally washed up, headed nowhere. We're talkin' about God's wisdom, a secret that's been hidden away, the kind He planned out before the universe even existed, all for our glory. None of those so-called rulers had a clue about it -- 'cause if they'd known, no way would they have crucified the Lord of glory. Bogus move on their part, big time.

web_text:
But as it is written,
“Things which an eye didn’t see, and an ear didn’t hear,
which didn’t enter into the heart of man,
these God has prepared for those who love him.” But to us, God revealed them through the Spirit. For the Spirit searches all things, yes, the deep things of God. For who among men knows the things of a man, except the spirit of the man, which is in him? Even so, no one knows the things of God, except God’s Spirit. But we received, not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit which is from God, that we might know the things that were freely given to us by God.
slang_text: these are the epic things God's got stashed away for the ones who love Him." But us? God let us in on it through the Spirit. 'Cause the Spirit's checkin' out everything, even the deepest, most gnarly stuff about God Himself. I mean, who's got the inside scoop on what's goin' on inside a dude except that dude's own spirit? Same deal here -- nobody's got the lowdown on God's business except God's own Spirit. And we didn't get handed the world's bogus vibe -- we got the real Spirit straight from God, so we could finally clue in on all the choice stuff He's freely given us.

web_text:
Which things also we speak, not in words which man’s wisdom teaches, but which the Holy Spirit teaches, comparing spiritual things with spiritual things. Now the natural man doesn’t receive the things of God’s Spirit, for they are foolishness to him, and he can’t know them, because they are spiritually discerned. But he who is spiritual discerns all things, and he himself is judged by no one. “For who has known the mind of the Lord, that he should instruct him?”
 But we have Christ’s mind.
slang_text: And that's exactly what we're talkin' about -- not usin' words some human brainiac cooked up, but words the Holy Spirit Himself is teachin' us, matchin' spiritual stuff up with spiritual stuff. Now your average dude who's just runnin' on natural instincts? He totally can't receive the things of God's Spirit -- to him it sounds like a bunch of grody nonsense, and he can't wrap his head around it 'cause you gotta discern it spiritually, dude. But the one who's plugged into the Spirit can suss out everything, and nobody can sit there judgin' him. "'Cause who's ever known the mind of the Lord well enough to school Him?" But us? We're rockin' the mind of Christ. For real.

web_text: Brothers, I couldn’t speak to you as to spiritual, but as to fleshly, as to babies in Christ. I fed you with milk, not with meat; for you weren’t yet ready. Indeed, not even now are you ready, for you are still fleshly. For insofar as there is jealousy, strife, and factions among you, aren’t you fleshly, and don’t you walk in the ways of men? For when one says, “I follow Paul,” and another, “I follow Apollos,” aren’t you fleshly?
slang_text: Look, dudes, I couldn't kick it to you like you were spiritual heavy-hitters -- nah, you were still fleshly, straight-up babies in Christ. I had to feed you baby formula, not a righteous steak dinner, 'cause you weren't ready for the real grub. And check it, you're still not ready, still stuck in fleshly mode. 'Cause when you're all trippin' with jealousy and beef and picking sides, that's grody, fleshly stuff, just acting like regular joes off the street. When one of you says "I'm Team Paul" and another goes "I'm Team Apollos," that's totally bogus behavior, no duh.

web_text: Who then is Apollos, and who is Paul, but servants through whom you believed; and each as the Lord gave to him? I planted. Apollos watered. But God gave the increase. So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the increase. Now he who plants and he who waters are the same, but each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
slang_text: So what's the big whoop about Apollos, or about me? We're just servants the Lord used to get you believing, each of us doing our own gig like the Lord handed us. I planted the seeds, Apollos watered 'em, but it was God who made the whole tubular thing grow. So no duh, the planter ain't nobody special and neither's the waterer -- it's God who makes it happen, like a boss. Planter and waterer, we're on the same team, and each of us gets our own reward based on how hard we hustled.

web_text: For we are God’s fellow workers. You are God’s farming, God’s building. According to the grace of God which was given to me, as a wise master builder I laid a foundation, and another builds on it. But let each man be careful how he builds on it. For no one can lay any other foundation than that which has been laid, which is Jesus Christ. But if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, costly stones, wood, hay, or stubble;
slang_text: 'Cause we're all just crew members working alongside God -- you're God's field, you're God's building project. By the grace God dished out to me, I laid down the foundation like a righteous master builder, and now somebody else is building on top of it. But everybody better watch how they're building, get a clue. Nobody can lay down some other foundation, 'cause the one already down is Jesus Christ, period. But if a dude builds on that foundation with gold, silver, and choice stones, or with wood, hay, and cheap stubble;

web_text: each man’s work will be revealed. For the Day will declare it, because it is revealed in fire; and the fire itself will test what sort of work each man’s work is. If any man’s work remains which he built on it, he will receive a reward. If any man’s work is burned, he will suffer loss, but he himself will be saved, but as through fire. Don’t you know that you are a temple of God, and that God’s Spirit lives in you?
slang_text: everybody's work is gonna get put on blast eventually. The big Day's gonna spill the truth, 'cause it all gets revealed in fire, and that fire's gonna test what kind of work each dude actually built -- was it righteous or was it bogus. If what somebody built survives, they score the reward. If it goes up in flames, they take the loss, but they themselves still get saved, just like barely scraping outta a fire. Don't you clueless dudes get it -- you ARE God's temple, and God's own Spirit is chillin' inside you?

web_text: If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy him; for God’s temple is holy, which you are. Let no one deceive himself. If anyone thinks that he is wise among you in this world, let him become a fool, that he may become wise. For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, “He has taken the wise in their craftiness.” And again, “The Lord knows the reasoning of the wise, that it is worthless.”
slang_text: If anybody trashes God's temple, God's gonna trash them right back, no joke, 'cause God's temple is holy, and that's exactly what you are. Don't nobody go fooling themselves. If some dipstick thinks he's the smartest cookie in the world's eyes, he better become a fool instead, so he can actually get wise for real. 'Cause this world's so-called wisdom is straight-up foolishness to God. Like it's written: "He catches the so-called wise dudes in their own sneaky schemes." And again: "The Lord knows exactly what the wise are thinkin', and it's totally worthless."

web_text: Therefore let no one boast in men. For all things are yours, whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come. All are yours, and you are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s.
slang_text: So don't nobody go bragging on mere dudes, get a clue. 'Cause everything's already yours -- Paul, Apollos, Cephas, this whole world, life, death, what's happening now, what's coming down the pike -- it's all yours, hella bodacious as that sounds. And you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God. Fer sure.

web_text: So let a man think of us as Christ’s servants, and stewards of God’s mysteries. Here, moreover, it is required of stewards, that they be found faithful. But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you, or by man’s judgment. Yes, I don’t judge my own self. For I know nothing against myself. Yet I am not justified by this, but he who judges me is the Lord.
slang_text: So dudes oughta think of us as Christ's servants, the crew handed the keys to God's deepest secrets. Now here's the deal on stewards -- you gotta be found faithful, no duh. As for me, it's totally no biggie if you or any human jury wanna judge me. Heck, I don't even judge myself, 'cause my own conscience isn't nagging me about anything. But that doesn't get me off the hook -- the Lord's the one who really judges me, fer sure.

web_text: Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord comes, who will both bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and reveal the counsels of the hearts. Then each man will get his praise from God. Now these things, brothers, I have in a figure transferred to myself and Apollos for your sakes, that in us you might learn not to think beyond the things which are written, that none of you be puffed up against one another. For who makes you different? And what do you have that you didn’t receive? But if you did receive it, why do you boast as if you had not received it? You are already filled. You have already become rich. You have come to reign without us. Yes, and I wish that you did reign, that we also might reign with you.
slang_text: So don't go trippin' and judging stuff before it's time -- wait for the Lord to show up, 'cause he's gonna flip the lights on every shady secret and expose what's really cookin' in people's hearts. Then everybody gets their props straight from God. Now, this whole bit, I've been running it through me and Apollos just for your sake, so you can get a clue from us not to go past what's written, and not get all puffed up playing favorites. 'Cause who made you so special, huh? What have you got that you didn't get handed to you? And if you did get it handed to you, why are you braggin' like a boss like you earned it yourself? Oh snap, look at you -- already stuffed, already filthy rich, already ruling like kings without us! I'm so sure. Man, I wish you really were ruling, so we could rule right alongside you.

web_text: For, I think that God has displayed us, the apostles, last of all, like men sentenced to death. For we are made a spectacle to the world, both to angels and men. We are fools for Christ’s sake, but you are wise in Christ. We are weak, but you are strong. You have honor, but we have dishonor. Even to this present hour we hunger, thirst, are naked, are beaten, and have no certain dwelling place. We toil, working with our own hands. When people curse us, we bless. Being persecuted, we endure.
slang_text: 'Cause I figure God's put us apostles dead last in the lineup, like a buncha wastoids sentenced to die -- we're the sideshow for the whole world to gawk at, angels and dudes alike. We're the spazzes for Christ's sake, but you're all so wise in Christ. We're weak, but you're so strong. You get the glory, we get grody looks. Right up to this hour we're hungry, thirsty, going around in rags, getting beat on, with no place to even crash. We work our tails off with our own two hands. People cuss us out, we bless 'em back. They come after us, and we just take it, no complaints.

web_text: Being defamed, we entreat. We are made as the filth of the world, the dirt wiped off by all, even until now. I don’t write these things to shame you, but to admonish you as my beloved children. For though you have ten thousand tutors in Christ, you don’t have many fathers. For in Christ Jesus, I became your father through the Good News. I beg you therefore, be imitators of me.
slang_text: They trash-talk us, we just keep beggin' folks nicely. We've basically become the scum of the earth, the garbage everybody wipes off their shoes, and that's still going on right now, no lie. I'm not writing this to make you feel like grody dipsticks -- I'm laying it on you like your own dad, 'cause I love you. See, you might have ten thousand tutors bossing you around in Christ, but you don't got a whole lotta real fathers. I'm the one who became your father in Christ Jesus, through the Good News, fer sure. So I'm begging you, dudes -- be like me.

web_text: Because of this I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, who will remind you of my ways which are in Christ, even as I teach everywhere in every assembly. Now some are puffed up, as though I were not coming to you. But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord is willing. And I will know, not the word of those who are puffed up, but the power. For God’s Kingdom is not in word, but in power.
slang_text: That's why I bounced Timothy over your way -- my beloved and totally faithful kid in the Lord, who's gonna remind you how I roll in Christ, same stuff I teach everywhere, in every single church. Now some of you are getting all puffed up, actin' like I'm never gonna show. Well, guess what -- I'm bouncing your way real soon, if the Lord says the word. And when I get there, I ain't checking out the fancy talk of these puffed-up dudes, I'm checking their actual power. 'Cause God's Kingdom isn't just words, it's the real deal, hella power.

web_text: What do you want? Shall I come to you with a rod, or in love and a spirit of gentleness?
slang_text: So what's it gonna be? You want me rollin' up with a stick, ready to lay down the law, or comin' in chill, full of love and a gentle spirit? Your call, dudes.

web_text: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and such sexual immorality as is not even named among the Gentiles, that one has his father’s wife. You are puffed up, and didn’t rather mourn, that he who had done this deed might be removed from among you. For I most certainly, as being absent in body but present in spirit, have already, as though I were present, judged him who has done this thing. In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, you being gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,
slang_text: Word's actually gotten around that there's some grody sexual immorality going down in your crew -- stuff so bogus even the pagans wouldn't touch it, some dude sleeping with his own dad's wife. And you're all puffed up and proud, when you should've been bummed out and bawling, so this guy could get bounced outta the group. 'Cause for real, even though I'm not there in the flesh, I'm there in spirit, and I've already made my call on this dude like I was standing right there. In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, with y'all gathered up and my spirit right there with you, backed by the power of our Lord Jesus Christ --

web_text: are to deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus. Your boasting is not good. Don’t you know that a little yeast leavens the whole lump? Purge out the old yeast, that you may be a new lump, even as you are unleavened. For indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed in our place. Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old yeast, neither with the yeast of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
slang_text: -- hand the guy over to Satan to wreck his flesh, so his spirit still gets saved on the day of the Lord Jesus. Your bragging is bogus, dudes, not good at all. Don't you get it -- a little bit of yeast puffs up the whole batch of dough? Purge out that old yeast, so you can be a fresh new batch, seeing as you're already unleavened deep down. 'Cause Christ, our Passover lamb, already got sacrificed for us, for real. So let's keep the party going, not with old nasty yeast, not with the yeast of spite and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

web_text: I wrote to you in my letter to have no company with sexual sinners; yet not at all meaning with the sexual sinners of this world, or with the covetous and extortionists, or with idolaters; for then you would have to leave the world. But as it is, I wrote to you not to associate with anyone who is called a brother who is a sexual sinner, or covetous, or an idolater, or a slanderer, or a drunkard, or an extortionist. Don’t even eat with such a person. For what do I have to do with also judging those who are outside? Don’t you judge those who are within?
slang_text: I wrote you guys in my last letter not to hang with sexual sinners -- but no way did I mean the sinners out there in the whole world, the greedy dipsticks and con artists and idol-worshipers, 'cause then you'd have to totally bounce outta the planet. What I actually meant was this: don't kick it with anybody who calls himself a brother but is a sexual sinner, or greedy, or into idols, or trash-talks people, or a drunk, or a con artist. Don't even sit down to eat with a spazoid like that. I mean, it's not my job to judge the outsiders, right? Isn't it the folks inside the church you're supposed to be judging?

web_text: But those who are outside, God judges. “Put away the wicked man from among yourselves.”
slang_text: But those outside the church, God's the one who judges them. Like it says, "Kick that wicked dude outta your crew." No duh.

web_text: Dare any of you, having a matter against his neighbor, go to law before the unrighteous, and not before the saints? Don’t you know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is judged by you, are you unworthy to judge the smallest matters? Don’t you know that we will judge angels? How much more, things that pertain to this life? If then, you have to judge things pertaining to this life, do you set them to judge who are of no account in the assembly?
slang_text: No way, are you seriously dragging your brother to some bogus courtroom full of dudes who don't even know God, instead of sorting it out with the saints? Get a clue -- don't you know we're the ones who are gonna judge the whole world someday? And if you're gonna judge the world, what's your damage that you can't handle some piddly little squabble? Don't you know we're even gonna judge angels, for real? So how much more this everyday stuff? If you've gotta settle this junk anyway, why are you handing it to people the church wouldn't even trust with a chore?

web_text: I say this to move you to shame. Isn’t there even one wise man among you who would be able to decide between his brothers? But brother goes to law with brother, and that before unbelievers! Therefore it is already altogether a defect in you, that you have lawsuits one with another. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded? No, but you yourselves do wrong, and defraud, and that against your brothers.
slang_text: I'm saying this straight up to make you blush a little. Is there seriously not one wise dude in your whole crew who could settle stuff between brothers? Instead brother's hauling brother into court, and in front of total unbelievers, no less -- that's bogus. Real talk, it's already a major fail on your part that you're even suing each other. Why not just take the hit? Why not just let yourself get ripped off? But no, you're the ones doing the wronging and the ripping off, and you're doing it to your own brothers. Way harsh.

web_text: Or don’t you know that the unrighteous will not inherit God’s Kingdom? Don’t be deceived. Neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor male prostitutes, nor homosexuals, nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor slanderers, nor extortionists, will inherit God’s Kingdom. Such were some of you, but you were washed. But you were sanctified. But you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and in the Spirit of our God. “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are expedient. “All things are lawful for me,” but I will not be brought under the power of anything.
slang_text: Or don't you know the unrighteous flat-out don't get in on God's Kingdom? No duh, but don't go trippin' and letting yourself get fooled about it. Sexually immoral folks, idol-worshippers, cheaters, male prostitutes, guys sleeping with guys, thieves, greedy dipsticks, drunks, trash-talkers, con artists -- none of 'em inherit God's Kingdom. Fer sure. And some of you dudes used to be exactly that grody. But check it -- you got washed clean, you got set apart, you got made right with God, all in the name of the Lord Jesus and by the Spirit of our God. So yeah, "I can do whatever I want," but not everything's actually good for you. "I can do whatever I want," but I'm not gonna let anything boss me around and turn me into its wastoid.

web_text: “Foods for the belly, and the belly for foods,” but God will bring to nothing both it and them. But the body is not for sexual immorality, but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body. Now God raised up the Lord, and will also raise us up by his power. Don’t you know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ, and make them members of a prostitute? May it never be! Or don’t you know that he who is joined to a prostitute is one body? For, “The two”, he says, “will become one flesh.”
slang_text: Sure, "food's for the stomach and the stomach's for food," but God's gonna wipe out both of 'em someday anyway. But your body isn't for hooking up with some grody prostitute -- it's for the Lord, and the Lord's totally into your body too. God raised the Lord up from the dead, and he's gonna raise us up the same way by his power, for real. Don't you know your bodies are actual parts of Christ himself? So am I supposed to take pieces of Christ and hook 'em up to some prostitute? As if! No way, don't have a cow, that's never happening. Don't you know that whoever hooks up with a prostitute becomes one body with her? It says right there, "the two become one flesh."

web_text: But he who is joined to the Lord is one spirit. Flee sexual immorality! “Every sin that a man does is outside the body,” but he who commits sexual immorality sins against his own body. Or don’t you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God? You are not your own, for you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God’s.
slang_text: But whoever's hooked up with the Lord becomes one spirit with him, totally tubular. So bounce, dudes -- bail on sexual immorality, don't even mess around with it! Every other sin a person does is kinda outside the body, but sexual immorality is a sin against your own body, straight up. Or don't you know your body's a temple of the Holy Spirit, who's living right inside you, given to you by God himself? You're not even your own, 'cause you got bought and paid for, price tag and all. So do the righteous thing and glorify God with your body and your spirit -- 'cause they belong to him, not you.

web_text: Now concerning the things about which you wrote to me: it is good for a man not to touch a woman. But, because of sexual immoralities, let each man have his own wife, and let each woman have her own husband. Let the husband give his wife the affection owed her, and likewise also the wife her husband. The wife doesn’t have authority over her own body, but the husband. Likewise also the husband doesn’t have authority over his own body, but the wife.
slang_text: Okay, so about that stuff you wrote me about -- for real, it's a righteous thing for a guy to stay solo and not mess around with a chick. But 'cause there's so much grody sexual immorality floatin' around out there, every dude oughta have his own wife, and every dudette her own husband. Fer sure. Husband, you gotta give your wife the affection she's owed, no half-steppin', and same goes for the wife back to her husband. Here's the deal, dudette -- your body ain't just yours to call the shots on, your husband's got a say too. And dude, same for you -- your body ain't just yours no more, your wife's got a say in it too.

web_text: Don’t deprive one another, unless it is by consent for a season, that you may give yourselves to fasting and prayer, and may be together again, that Satan doesn’t tempt you because of your lack of self-control. But this I say by way of concession, not of commandment. Yet I wish that all men were like me. However each man has his own gift from God, one of this kind, and another of that kind. But I say to the unmarried and to widows, it is good for them if they remain even as I am.
slang_text: Don't go holdin' out on each other -- unless you're both totally down with it for a chill season, so you can focus up on fasting and prayer, then get back together again. Otherwise Satan's gonna spot your lack of self-control and totally tempt you, and that's bogus news. Now I'm sayin' this as a nice option, dudes, not some hardcore commandment. Honestly I wish everybody could roll solo like me, but no duh, everybody's got their own gift from God -- one guy's wired this way, another guy's wired that way. But to the singles and the widows out there, I'm tellin' you it's righteous if you just stay chill like I am.

web_text: But if they don’t have self-control, let them marry. For it’s better to marry than to burn. But to the married I command—not I, but the Lord—that the wife not leave her husband (but if she departs, let her remain unmarried, or else be reconciled to her husband), and that the husband not leave his wife. But to the rest I—not the Lord—say, if any brother has an unbelieving wife, and she is content to live with him, let him not leave her.
slang_text: But if they can't hold it together, they better go ahead and get hitched -- it's way better to marry than to be burnin' up inside, no duh. Now to the married folks, I got a command -- and this ain't me talkin', this is straight from the Lord -- the wife shouldn't bounce on her husband (but if she does bounce, she's gotta stay single or patch things up with him), and the husband shouldn't bounce on his wife either. To everybody else, this part's me talkin', not the Lord -- if a brother's got a wife who's not a believer, and she's cool stickin' around with him, he shouldn't ditch her.

web_text: The woman who has an unbelieving husband, and he is content to live with her, let her not leave her husband. For the unbelieving husband is sanctified in the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified in the husband. Otherwise your children would be unclean, but now they are holy. Yet if the unbeliever departs, let there be separation. The brother or the sister is not under bondage in such cases, but God has called us in peace. For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband? Or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife?
slang_text: Same deal for a dudette with an unbelieving husband -- if he's cool stickin' around with her, she shouldn't bounce on him either. See, the unbelieving husband gets set apart through his wife, and the unbelieving wife gets set apart through her husband -- otherwise your kids would be grody and unclean, but nope, they're holy, fer sure. But if the unbeliever wants out and bounces, let 'em go, no drama. The brother or sister ain't chained down in cases like that -- God called us to live in peace, not to have a cow over it. 'Cause dudette, how do you even know if you're gonna save your husband? Or dude, how do you know if you're gonna save your wife?

web_text: Only, as the Lord has distributed to each man, as God has called each, so let him walk. So I command in all the assemblies. Was anyone called having been circumcised? Let him not become uncircumcised. Has anyone been called in uncircumcision? Let him not be circumcised. Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but the keeping of the commandments of God. Let each man stay in that calling in which he was called.
slang_text: Bottom line -- whatever situation the Lord dealt you, whatever God called you into, just walk it out, dude. That's the word I'm droppin' in every single church, no exceptions. Was somebody called already circumcised? Don't go tryin' to undo it. Somebody called uncircumcised? Don't go gettin' circumcised now, that's trippin'. Circumcised or not, that stuff's nothing -- what matters, like a boss, is keepin' God's commandments. Everybody just stay put in whatever calling you got called into.

web_text: Were you called being a bondservant? Don’t let that bother you, but if you get an opportunity to become free, use it. For he who was called in the Lord being a bondservant is the Lord’s free man. Likewise he who was called being free is Christ’s bondservant. You were bought with a price. Don’t become bondservants of men. Brothers, let each man, in whatever condition he was called, stay in that condition with God.
slang_text: Were you a servant when God called you? Don't sweat it, don't have a cow -- but hey, if you get a shot to go free, take it, no duh. 'Cause the dude who got called in the Lord while still a servant, he's actually the Lord's free man on the inside. And same goes the other way -- the guy called while free, he's Christ's servant. You got bought, and it cost a price, so don't go turnin' yourselves into servants of mere people. Brothers, whatever condition you were in when you got called, stay chill in that condition, right there with God.

web_text: Now concerning virgins, I have no commandment from the Lord, but I give my judgment as one who has obtained mercy from the Lord to be trustworthy. Therefore I think that because of the distress that is on us, that it’s good for a man to remain as he is. Are you bound to a wife? Don’t seek to be freed. Are you free from a wife? Don’t seek a wife. But if you marry, you have not sinned. If a virgin marries, she has not sinned. Yet such will have oppression in the flesh, and I want to spare you.
slang_text: Now about the virgins out there -- I ain't got a straight-up commandment from the Lord on this one, but I'll give you my two cents, seein' as the Lord showed me mercy and made me somebody you can trust. So here's my take -- with all the rough stuff goin' down these days, it's a righteous move for a guy to just stay as he is. You got a wife already? Don't go seekin' to get loose. You're single? Don't go huntin' for a wife. But hey, if you do marry, that ain't no sin, no way. If a virgin marries, she ain't sinnin' either. It's just that married folks are gonna catch some extra hassle in this life, and I'm tryin' to spare you that grief.

web_text: But I say this, brothers: the time is short, that from now on, both those who have wives may be as though they had none; and those who weep, as though they didn’t weep; and those who rejoice, as though they didn’t rejoice; and those who buy, as though they didn’t possess; and those who use the world, as not using it to the fullest. For the mode of this world passes away. But I desire to have you to be free from cares. He who is unmarried is concerned for the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord;
slang_text: Here's the deal, brothers -- the clock's totally runnin' out, so from here on, guys with wives oughta live like they ain't even got one; folks who are cryin' should act like they ain't cryin'; folks who are stoked should act like they ain't stoked; folks buyin' stuff should act like they don't even own it; and folks usin' the world's stuff shouldn't go all in on it. 'Cause this whole world scene as we know it is fadin' out, fer sure. I want y'all free from worry and stress. A single guy's got his mind on the Lord's business, on how he can please the Lord;

web_text: but he who is married is concerned about the things of the world, how he may please his wife. There is also a difference between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman cares about the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit. But she who is married cares about the things of the world—how she may please her husband. This I say for your own profit; not that I may ensnare you, but for that which is appropriate, and that you may attend to the Lord without distraction. But if any man thinks that he is behaving inappropriately toward his virgin, if she is past the flower of her age, and if need so requires, let him do what he desires. He doesn’t sin. Let them marry.
slang_text: but a married guy's got his mind on worldly stuff, on how he can please his wife. Same split goes for a wife versus a virgin -- the single dudette cares about the Lord's business, keepin' herself holy in body and spirit both. But the married dudette cares about worldly stuff -- how she can please her husband. I'm sayin' all this for your own good, dudes, not to trap you or box you in, but 'cause it's the right, sensible move, so you can keep your eyes on the Lord without gettin' distracted. But if some guy figures he's actin' bogus toward his virgin -- like if she's past her prime years and the situation calls for it -- let him do what he's gotta do, that ain't no sin. Let 'em go ahead and get married.

web_text: But he who stands steadfast in his heart, having no urgency, but has power over his own will, and has determined in his own heart to keep his own virgin, does well. So then both he who gives his own virgin in marriage does well, and he who doesn’t give her in marriage does better. A wife is bound by law for as long as her husband lives; but if the husband is dead, she is free to be married to whomever she desires, only in the Lord. But she is happier if she stays as she is, in my judgment, and I think that I also have God’s Spirit.
slang_text: But the guy who's rock-solid in his heart, ain't got no pressure on him, and has full say over his own will, and has straight-up decided in his own heart to keep his virgin as she is -- he's doin' good. So the guy who gives his virgin away in marriage is doin' good, and the guy who doesn't give her away is doin' even better, fer sure. A wife's bound by law as long as her husband's still kickin'; but if the husband kicks the bucket, she's free to marry whoever she wants, long as it's in the Lord. But she's happier if she just stays as she is, that's my two cents, and I'm pretty sure I got God's Spirit backin' me up on this one too.But the guy who's rock-solid in his heart, ain't feelin' no pressure, and's got his own will totally under control, and's decided deep down to keep his virgin as she is -- he's doin' good, like a boss. So the dude who lets his virgin get married is doin' good, and the dude who doesn't is doin' even better. A wife's tied by the law as long as her husband's alive; but if he kicks the bucket, she's free to marry whoever she wants -- long as it's somebody in the Lord. But honestly, my two cents is she's happier stayin' as she is, and I'm pretty sure I got God's Spirit sayin' the same thing.

web_text: Now concerning things sacrificed to idols: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up. But if anyone thinks that he knows anything, he doesn’t yet know as he ought to know. But if anyone loves God, the same is known by him. Therefore concerning the eating of things sacrificed to idols, we know that no idol is anything in the world, and that there is no other God but one.
slang_text: Now about that meat sacrificed to idols -- we all know we've got the knowledge, fer sure. But get a clue: knowledge just puffs you up like a big ol' balloon, while love actually builds people up. If some dude thinks he's got it all figured out, he doesn't even know yet what he oughta know. But if somebody loves God, that person's known by God, no doubt. So about eatin' meat sacrificed to idols -- we know an idol ain't nothin' but bogus air in this whole world, and there's no other God but the one, period.

web_text: For though there are things that are called “gods”, whether in the heavens or on earth; as there are many “gods” and many “lords”; yet to us there is one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and we live through him. However, that knowledge isn’t in all men. But some, with consciousness of the idol until now, eat as of a thing sacrificed to an idol, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled. But food will not commend us to God. For neither, if we don’t eat, are we the worse; nor, if we eat, are we the better.
slang_text: Sure, there's stuff people call "gods" out there, up in the sky or down on earth -- tons of so-called "gods" and "lords." But for us, it's just one God, the Father, who everything comes from and who we live for, and one Lord, Jesus Christ, who everything comes through and who we live through. But not everybody's got that knowledge locked in yet. Some folks, still hung up on the idol thing till now, eat that meat like it's actually sacrificed to somethin' real, and their weak conscience gets grody and defiled. But food ain't gonna score us any points with God -- we're not worse off if we skip it, and we're not better off if we eat it, straight up.

web_text: But be careful that by no means does this liberty of yours become a stumbling block to the weak. For if a man sees you who have knowledge sitting in an idol’s temple, won’t his conscience, if he is weak, be emboldened to eat things sacrificed to idols? And through your knowledge, he who is weak perishes, the brother for whose sake Christ died. Thus, sinning against the brothers, and wounding their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.
slang_text: But watch out -- don't let your freedom here turn into a bogus stumblin' block for somebody weaker. 'Cause if some dude sees you, knowledge and all, chillin' in an idol's temple, won't his weak conscience get emboldened to go eat that idol-sacrificed meat too? And now, thanks to your "knowledge," this weak brother's goin' down -- the same brother Christ died for. So when you sin against your brothers like that and wound their weak conscience, you're actually sinnin' against Christ himself. Way harsh.

web_text: Therefore if food causes my brother to stumble, I will eat no meat forever more, that I don’t cause my brother to stumble.
slang_text: So bottom line -- if some food's gonna make my brother stumble, I ain't touchin' meat ever again, no way, no how, so I don't end up trippin' my brother up.

web_text: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Haven’t I seen Jesus Christ, our Lord? Aren’t you my work in the Lord? If to others I am not an apostle, yet at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord. My defense to those who examine me is this. Have we no right to eat and to drink?
slang_text: Am I not free, dude? Am I not an apostle, for real? Haven't I straight-up seen Jesus Christ our Lord with my own eyes? Aren't you guys literally my work in the Lord? Even if I'm not an apostle to other folks, I'm sure as heck one to you -- you're the seal on my apostleship, no doubt about it. Here's my comeback to anybody grillin' me on this: don't we even have the right to eat and drink like normal dudes?

web_text: Have we no right to take along a wife who is a believer, even as the rest of the apostles, and the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas? Or have only Barnabas and I no right to not work? What soldier ever serves at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard, and doesn’t eat of its fruit? Or who feeds a flock, and doesn’t drink from the flock’s milk? Do I speak these things according to the ways of men? Or doesn’t the law also say the same thing?
slang_text: Don't we have the right to bring along a believing wife, same as the rest of the apostles, and the Lord's brothers, and Cephas? Or is it just me and Barnabas who gotta hustle a day job on the side? What soldier out there ever pays his own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesn't get to eat any of the grapes? Or who's feedin' a flock and doesn't get to drink any of the milk? Am I just talkin' regular-people logic here, or doesn't the law say the exact same thing, fer sure?

web_text: For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain.” Is it for the oxen that God cares, or does he say it assuredly for our sake? Yes, it was written for our sake, because he who plows ought to plow in hope, and he who threshes in hope should partake of his hope. If we sowed to you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your fleshly things? If others partake of this right over you, don’t we yet more? Nevertheless we did not use this right, but we bear all things, that we may cause no hindrance to the Good News of Christ.
slang_text: 'Cause it's written right there in Moses's law: "Don't muzzle the ox while it's grindin' out the grain." Is God even trippin' about oxen here, or is he sayin' it for our sake, no duh? Yeah, it was written for us, 'cause the guy plowin' oughta plow with hope, and the guy threshin' oughta get a share of what he's hopin' for. If we planted spiritual stuff in you guys, is it really such a big deal if we reap some of your material stuff back? If other dudes get to claim this right over you, shouldn't we get it even more? But check it -- we never even used this right. We put up with everything instead, so we don't throw any bogus roadblocks in front of Christ's Good News.

web_text: Don’t you know that those who serve around sacred things eat from the things of the temple, and those who wait on the altar have their portion with the altar? Even so the Lord ordained that those who proclaim the Good News should live from the Good News. But I have used none of these things, and I don’t write these things that it may be done so in my case; for I would rather die, than that anyone should make my boasting void. For if I preach the Good News, I have nothing to boast about; for necessity is laid on me; but woe is to me, if I don’t preach the Good News.
slang_text: Don't you know that the folks servin' around sacred stuff eat outta the temple's supplies, and the ones tendin' the altar get their cut from the altar too? Same deal -- the Lord set it up so that folks preachin' the Good News get to live off the Good News. But check it, I never used any of that for myself, and I'm not even writin' this so it'll happen for me now -- I'd rather flat-out die than let anybody make my bragging rights worthless. 'Cause if I preach the Good News, that ain't nothin' to brag about -- I gotta do it, it's laid on me, no way around it. But it's way harsh for me if I don't preach the Good News.

web_text: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward. But if not of my own will, I have a stewardship entrusted to me. What then is my reward? That, when I preach the Good News, I may present the Good News of Christ without charge, so as not to abuse my authority in the Good News. For though I was free from all, I brought myself under bondage to all, that I might gain the more. To the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain Jews; to those who are under the law, as under the law, that I might gain those who are under the law;
slang_text: 'Cause if I'm doin' this outta my own free will, I got a reward comin'. But if it's not my own choice, I still got a job entrusted to me anyway. So what's my actual reward then? That when I preach the Good News, I get to hand out Christ's Good News for free, no charge, so I don't go abusin' my authority in this whole Good News gig. See, even though I was free from everybody, I made myself a servant to everybody, so I could win over more folks. To the Jews I acted like a Jew, so I could win Jews over; to the ones under the law, I acted like I was under the law too, so I could win over the ones under the law;

web_text: to those who are without law, as without law (not being without law toward God, but under law toward Christ), that I might win those who are without law. To the weak I became as weak, that I might gain the weak. I have become all things to all men, that I may by all means save some. Now I do this for the sake of the Good News, that I may be a joint partaker of it. Don’t you know that those who run in a race all run, but one receives the prize? Run like that, that you may win.
slang_text: to the folks without the law, I acted like I was without the law too (not that I'm without law toward God -- I'm totally under Christ's law), so I could win over the law-less crowd. To the weak, I became weak, so I could win over the weak. Basically I've become all things to all people, so I can save some of 'em by any means possible. I'm doin' all this for the Good News's sake, so I get to share in it too. Don't you know that in a race, everybody runs, but only one dude actually gets the prize? Run like that, dude -- run to win.

web_text: Every man who strives in the games exercises self-control in all things. Now they do it to receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible. I therefore run like that, not aimlessly. I fight like that, not beating the air, but I beat my body and bring it into submission, lest by any means, after I have preached to others, I myself should be rejected.
slang_text: Every dude competin' in the games keeps total self-control in everything, no exceptions. And they're doin' it just to score some crown that's gonna fall apart anyway, but we're runnin' for one that never fades, fer sure. So that's how I run -- not just aimlessly wanderin' around. That's how I fight too -- not just punchin' at thin air. I beat my own body down and keep it in check, so that after I've preached to everybody else, I don't end up gettin' rejected myself. Way harsh, but real talk.

web_text: Now I would not have you ignorant, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea; and were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea; and all ate the same spiritual food; and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
slang_text: Now brothers, I don't want you clueless on this one -- our forefathers were all under that cloud, and they all made it through the sea; they all got baptized into Moses right there in the cloud and the sea; they all ate the same spiritual chow, and they all drank the same spiritual drink. 'Cause they drank from a spiritual rock that was straight-up followin' them around, and that rock was Christ, no lie.

web_text: However with most of them, God was not well pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness. Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted. Don’t be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.” Let us not commit sexual immorality, as some of them committed, and in one day twenty-three thousand fell.
slang_text: But most of 'em, God wasn't stoked on at all -- they got wiped out in the wilderness, way harsh. Now these things happened as examples for us, so we don't go lustin' after evil stuff the way they did. Don't be idol-worshippers like some of 'em were. Like it's written, "The people sat down to eat and drink, then got up and partied hard." Let's not commit sexual immorality like some of 'em did, and in one single day, twenty-three thousand of 'em dropped dead. No lie.

web_text: Let us not test Christ, as some of them tested, and perished by the serpents. Don’t grumble, as some of them also grumbled, and perished by the destroyer. Now all these things happened to them by way of example, and they were written for our admonition, on whom the ends of the ages have come. Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful that he doesn’t fall.
slang_text: Let's not go testin' Christ like some of 'em did, and got wasted by the snakes. Don't go grumblin' either, like some of 'em grumbled, and got wasted by the destroyer. Now all this stuff happened to 'em as an example, and it got written down as a warning for us, since we're the ones the ages are windin' down on. So whoever thinks they're standin' tall better watch out, so they don't totally fall.

web_text: No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it. Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry. I speak as to wise men. Judge what I say. The cup of blessing which we bless, isn’t it a sharing of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, isn’t it a sharing of the body of Christ?
slang_text: No temptation's hit you except the totally normal kind everybody deals with. God's faithful, fer sure -- he won't let you get tempted past what you can handle, and he'll always give you a way outta it, so you can hang tough through it. So bounce, my dudes -- flee from idol-worship. I'm talkin' to you like you're smart folks; judge for yourselves what I'm sayin'. The cup of blessing we bless, isn't that us sharin' in Christ's blood? The bread we break, isn't that us sharin' in Christ's body?

web_text: Because there is one loaf of bread, we, who are many, are one body; for we all partake of the one loaf of bread. Consider Israel according to the flesh. Don’t those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar? What am I saying then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything? But I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons, and not to God, and I don’t desire that you would have fellowship with demons.
slang_text: 'Cause there's one loaf of bread, so we, even though there's a ton of us, are one body -- we all get a piece of that one loaf, together. Check out Israel accordin' to the flesh -- don't the folks eatin' the sacrifices get in on the altar too? So what am I sayin' here? That an idol-sacrificed thing is actually somethin', or that an idol's actually somethin'? Nah. But I'm sayin' whatever the Gentiles sacrifice, they're sacrificin' it to demons, not to God, and I don't want you guys gettin' cozy with demons, no way.

web_text: You can’t both drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You can’t both partake of the table of the Lord, and of the table of demons. Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he? “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are profitable. “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things build up. Let no one seek his own, but each one his neighbor’s good.
slang_text: You can't drink the Lord's cup and demons' cup both, that's just bogus. You can't sit at the Lord's table and the demons' table both either. Or are we tryin' to make the Lord jealous? You think we're stronger than him? As if! "I can do whatever I want," sure, but not everything's actually profitable. "I can do whatever I want," but not everything builds people up. Nobody oughta just chase their own interests -- go after your neighbor's good instead.

web_text: Whatever is sold in the butcher shop, eat, asking no question for the sake of conscience, for “the earth is the Lord’s, and its fullness.” But if one of those who don’t believe invites you to a meal, and you are inclined to go, eat whatever is set before you, asking no questions for the sake of conscience. But if anyone says to you, “This was offered to idols,” don’t eat it for the sake of the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience. For “the earth is the Lord’s, and all its fullness.”
slang_text: Whatever's sold at the meat market, go ahead and eat it, no need to ask questions for conscience's sake, 'cause "the earth's the Lord's, and everything in it." But if some unbeliever invites you over for a meal, and you're down to go, eat whatever's put in front of you, no questions asked, for conscience's sake. But if somebody straight-up tells you, "This was offered to idols," don't eat it, outta respect for the person who told you, and for conscience's sake. 'Cause "the earth's the Lord's, and all that's in it."

web_text: Conscience, I say, not your own, but the other’s conscience. For why is my liberty judged by another conscience? If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced for something I give thanks for? Whether therefore you eat, or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God. Give no occasion for stumbling, either to Jews, or to Greeks, or to the assembly of God;
slang_text: Conscience, I'm sayin' -- not your own, but the other dude's conscience. 'Cause why should my freedom get judged by somebody else's conscience? If I'm eatin' with a thankful heart, why am I gettin' trash-talked for somethin' I'm literally thankin' God for? So whether you eat, drink, or whatever you're doin', do it all for God's glory, fer sure. Don't give anybody a reason to stumble, not Jews, not Greeks, not the church of God;

web_text: even as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of the many, that they may be saved.
slang_text: same way I try to please everybody in everything, not chasin' my own profit, but the profit of the crowd, so they can get saved. That's the whole deal, no lie.

web_text: Be imitators of me, even as I also am of Christ. Now I praise you, brothers, that you remember me in all things, and hold firm the traditions, even as I delivered them to you. But I would have you know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is man, and the head of Christ is God. Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.
slang_text: Be like me, dudes, same as I'm like Christ. Now I gotta give you props, brothers, for rememberin' me in everything and holdin' firm to the traditions I handed down to you, no duh. But I want y'all to know somethin' -- Christ is the head of every guy, the guy's the head of the woman, and God's the head of Christ. Any guy who prays or prophesies with his head covered up totally disrespects his own head.

web_text: But every woman praying or prophesying with her head uncovered dishonors her head. For it is one and the same thing as if she were shaved. For if a woman is not covered, let her hair also be cut off. But if it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut off or be shaved, let her be covered. For a man indeed ought not to have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God, but the woman is the glory of the man. For man is not from woman, but woman from man;
slang_text: But every woman prayin' or prophesyin' with her head uncovered disrespects her own head too -- same deal as if she'd shaved it clean off. If a woman won't cover up, she might as well go cut her hair off too. But if it's totally shameful for a woman to have her hair cut or shaved, then let her cover up, fer sure. A guy shouldn't cover his head, 'cause he's the image and glory of God, but the woman's the glory of the man. 'Cause man didn't come from woman, woman came from man;

web_text: for man wasn’t created for the woman, but woman for the man. For this cause the woman ought to have authority over her own head, because of the angels. Nevertheless, neither is the woman independent of the man, nor the man independent of the woman, in the Lord. For as woman came from man, so a man also comes through a woman; but all things are from God.
slang_text: and the guy wasn't made for the woman, the woman was made for the guy. That's why the woman oughta have authority over her own head, on account of the angels. But hold up -- in the Lord, the woman ain't independent from the man, and the man ain't independent from the woman either. 'Cause just like woman came from man, a man also comes through a woman -- but everything, all of it, comes from God.

web_text: Judge for yourselves. Is it appropriate that a woman pray to God unveiled? Doesn’t even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him? But if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her, for her hair is given to her for a covering. But if any man seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither do God’s assemblies.
slang_text: Judge for yourselves, dudes. Is it even cool for a woman to pray to God without a veil on? Doesn't nature itself teach you that if a guy's got long hair, that's grody and dishonoring to him? But if a woman's got long hair, that's her glory, 'cause her hair's given to her as a natural covering. But if any guy wants to argue this into the ground, we ain't got that custom, and neither do God's churches.

web_text: But in giving you this command, I don’t praise you, that you come together not for the better but for the worse. For first of all, when you come together in the assembly, I hear that divisions exist among you, and I partly believe it. For there also must be factions among you, that those who are approved may be revealed among you. When therefore you assemble yourselves together, it is not the Lord’s supper that you eat.
slang_text: But layin' this next command on you, I ain't givin' you props, 'cause when you get together, it's makin' things worse, not better. First off, when y'all meet up as a church, I hear there's divisions goin' on among you, and I halfway believe it, no lie. There's gotta be factions among you anyway, so the folks who are actually solid get shown for who they are. So when you all get together, what you're eatin' ain't even the Lord's Supper anymore.

web_text:
For in your eating each one takes his own supper first. One is hungry, and another is drunken. What, don’t you have houses to eat and to drink in? Or do you despise God’s assembly, and put them to shame who don’t have enough? What shall I tell you? Shall I praise you? In this I don’t praise you. For I received from the Lord that which also I delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread. When he had given thanks, he broke it, and said,
“Take, eat. This is my body, which is broken for you. Do this in memory of me.”
slang_text:
'Cause when you're eatin', everybody just grabs their own supper first -- one dude's starvin', another dude's straight-up drunk. What, don't you got houses to eat and drink in? Or are you dissin' God's church, shamin' the folks who don't have enough? What am I supposed to say to you? Should I give you props? No way, not for this. This is what I got from the Lord and handed down to you: on the night he got betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread. After he gave thanks, he broke it and said,
"Take this, eat it. This is my body, broken for you. Do this to remember me."

web_text:
In the same way he also took the cup, after supper, saying,
“This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink, in memory of me.” For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes. Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks the Lord’s cup in a way unworthy of the Lord will be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord. But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread, and drink of the cup.
slang_text:
Same way, he took the cup after supper too, and said,
"This cup is the new covenant, sealed in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me." 'Cause every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you're proclaimin' the Lord's death until he comes back. So whoever eats this bread or drinks the Lord's cup in a way that disrespects the Lord is gonna be guilty of his body and blood -- that's way harsh. So everybody better examine themselves first, and then eat the bread and drink the cup.

web_text: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy way eats and drinks judgment to himself, if he doesn’t discern the Lord’s body. For this cause many among you are weak and sickly, and not a few sleep. For if we discerned ourselves, we wouldn’t be judged. But when we are judged, we are punished by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.
slang_text: 'Cause whoever eats and drinks in a disrespectin' way is literally eatin' and drinkin' judgment on themselves, if they're not discernin' the Lord's body right. That's exactly why so many of you are weak and sickly, and quite a few of you have even died, no lie. If we actually examined ourselves, we wouldn't get judged like this. But when we do get judged, the Lord's disciplinin' us, so we don't end up condemned along with the rest of the world.

web_text: Therefore, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another. But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest your coming together be for judgment. The rest I will set in order whenever I come.
slang_text: So bottom line, my brothers, when you all get together to eat, wait up for each other, don't be dipsticks about it. But if somebody's starvin', let 'em eat at home first, so your gettin' together doesn't turn into a judgment thing. The rest of it, I'll sort out whenever I roll through.

web_text: Now concerning spiritual things, brothers, I don’t want you to be ignorant. You know that when you were heathen, you were led away to those mute idols, however you might be led. Therefore I make known to you that no man speaking by God’s Spirit says, “Jesus is accursed.” No one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” but by the Holy Spirit. Now there are various kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit.
slang_text: Now about spiritual stuff, brothers, I don't want you clueless on this one. You know back when you were heathens, you got dragged around after those mute idols, however you got dragged. So here's the deal -- nobody speakin' by God's Spirit ever says, "Jesus is cursed," no way. And nobody can say, "Jesus is Lord," except by the Holy Spirit, fer sure. Now there's all kinds of different gifts, but it's the same Spirit behind 'em all.

web_text: There are various kinds of service, and the same Lord. There are various kinds of workings, but the same God, who works all things in all. But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the profit of all. For to one is given through the Spirit the word of wisdom, and to another the word of knowledge, according to the same Spirit;
slang_text: There's all different kinds of service, but the same Lord runnin' the show. There's all different kinds of workings, but it's the same God workin' everything in everybody. But every single person gets some manifestation of the Spirit, and it's for everybody's benefit, not just their own. 'Cause one dude gets the word of wisdom through the Spirit, and another gets the word of knowledge, same Spirit both times;

web_text: to another faith, by the same Spirit; and to another gifts of healings, by the same Spirit; and to another workings of miracles; and to another prophecy; and to another discerning of spirits; to another different kinds of languages; and to another the interpretation of languages. But the one and the same Spirit produces all of these, distributing to each one separately as he desires. For as the body is one, and has many members, and all the members of the body, being many, are one body; so also is Christ.
slang_text: to another guy faith, same Spirit; to another guy healing gifts, same Spirit; to another guy the power to work miracles; to another prophecy; to another the ability to suss out spirits; to another different kinds of languages; and to another the ability to interpret languages. But it's one and the same Spirit doin' all this, handin' it out to each person exactly how he wants, no argument. 'Cause just like the body's one thing but's got a ton of parts, and all those parts, even though there's a bunch of 'em, make up one body -- same deal with Christ.

web_text: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether bond or free; and were all given to drink into one Spirit. For the body is not one member, but many. If the foot would say, “Because I’m not the hand, I’m not part of the body,” it is not therefore not part of the body. If the ear would say, “Because I’m not the eye, I’m not part of the body,” it’s not therefore not part of the body.
slang_text: 'Cause we all got baptized into one body by one Spirit, whether we're Jews or Greeks, slaves or free -- and we all got to drink of that same one Spirit, fer sure. The body ain't just one part, it's a ton of 'em. If the foot said, "'Cause I'm not the hand, I'm not part of the body," that doesn't make it any less part of the body, no way. If the ear said, "'Cause I'm not the eye, I'm not part of the body," same deal -- it's still part of the body.

web_text: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the smelling be? But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body, just as he desired. If they were all one member, where would the body be? But now they are many members, but one body.
slang_text: If the whole body was just one big eye, where'd the hearin' even be? If the whole thing was just hearin', where'd the smellin' be? But right now, God set every single part in the body exactly how he wanted it, no duh. If they were all just one part, where would the body even be? But nah, there's a ton of parts, and it's still just one body.

web_text: The eye can’t tell the hand, “I have no need for you,” or again the head to the feet, “I have no need for you.” No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary. Those parts of the body which we think to be less honorable, on those we bestow more abundant honor; and our unpresentable parts have more abundant propriety; whereas our presentable parts have no such need. But God composed the body together, giving more abundant honor to the inferior part,
slang_text: The eye can't tell the hand, "I don't need you," and the head can't tell the feet, "I don't need you" either, that's bogus. Nah, actually the parts of the body that seem weaker are totally necessary. The parts we figure are less honorable, we dress those up with even more honor; and our less presentable parts get treated with way more modesty, while our presentable parts don't need that treatment at all. But God put the body together on purpose, givin' more honor to the part that seems lower,

web_text: that there should be no division in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another. When one member suffers, all the members suffer with it. Or when one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it. Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually. God has set some in the assembly: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracle workers, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, and various kinds of languages.
slang_text: so there's no division in the body, and all the parts look out for each other the same way. When one part's hurtin', all the parts hurt right along with it. Or when one part gets honored, all the parts celebrate right along with it, fer sure. Now you guys are the body of Christ, and each one of you is a part of it individually. And God set some folks up in the church: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracle workers, then healing gifts, then helpers, then leaders, then different kinds of languages.

web_text: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all miracle workers? Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with various languages? Do all interpret? But earnestly desire the best gifts. Moreover, I show a most excellent way to you.
slang_text: Are all of you apostles? Are all of you prophets? Are all of you teachers? Are all of you miracle workers? Do all of you have healing gifts? Do all of you speak in different languages? Do all of you interpret? No duh, not everybody's the same. But go after the best gifts with everything you got. And on top of that, I'm gonna show you a way that's better than all of it, hands down.

web_text: If I speak with the languages of men and of angels, but don’t have love, I have become sounding brass, or a clanging cymbal. If I have the gift of prophecy, and know all mysteries and all knowledge; and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but don’t have love, I am nothing. If I give away all my goods to feed the poor, and if I give my body to be burned, but don’t have love, it profits me nothing. Love is patient and is kind; love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud,
slang_text: If I could speak every language out there, even the angels' own language, but I don't have love, I'm nothin' but a clangin' cymbal, just noise. If I had the gift of prophecy, and knew every mystery and every ounce of knowledge there is, and had faith enough to move whole mountains, but didn't have love, I'd be nothin', for real. If I gave away everything I own to feed the poor, and even handed my own body over to be burned, but didn't have love, none of it would do me a bit of good. Love's patient, love's kind. Love doesn't get jealous. Love doesn't brag, doesn't get puffed up,

web_text: doesn’t behave itself inappropriately, doesn’t seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil; doesn’t rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth; bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things. Love never fails. But where there are prophecies, they will be done away with. Where there are various languages, they will cease. Where there is knowledge, it will be done away with.
slang_text: doesn't act shady, doesn't chase after just its own way, doesn't fly off the handle, doesn't keep score of wrongs. Love doesn't get stoked over bogus, wrong stuff -- it gets stoked over the truth instead. It carries everything, believes everything, hopes for everything, sticks it out through everything. Love never falls apart, no matter what. But prophecies? They're gonna fade away someday. Speakin' in different languages? That's gonna stop. Knowledge? That's gonna fade away too.

web_text: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part; but when that which is complete has come, then that which is partial will be done away with. When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I felt as a child, I thought as a child. Now that I have become a man, I have put away childish things. For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part, but then I will know fully, even as I was also fully known.
