slang_text: 'Cause right now we only know part of the picture, and we only prophesy part of the picture too -- but once the complete thing shows up, all that partial stuff's gonna fade away, no doubt. When I was a little kid, I talked like a kid, I felt like a kid, I thought like a kid. But now that I'm grown, I've put all that childish stuff away for good. Right now we're seein' everything like lookin' through a foggy, beat-up mirror, but someday it's gonna be face to face, totally clear. Right now I only know part of it, but someday I'm gonna know everything fully, just like I've been fully known the whole time.

web_text: But now faith, hope, and love remain—these three. The greatest of these is love.
slang_text: But for now, three things stick around forever: faith, hope, and love. And the biggest one of the three, hands down, no contest -- that's love.

web_text: Follow after love, and earnestly desire spiritual gifts, but especially that you may prophesy. For he who speaks in another language speaks not to men, but to God; for no one understands; but in the Spirit he speaks mysteries. But he who prophesies speaks to men for their edification, exhortation, and consolation. He who speaks in another language edifies himself, but he who prophesies edifies the assembly.
slang_text: Chase after love, and go after spiritual gifts hard, but especially prophecy. 'Cause whoever speaks in another language isn't talkin' to people, he's talkin' to God -- nobody gets it, he's just speakin' mysteries by the Spirit. But whoever prophesies is talkin' to people, buildin' 'em up, encouragin' 'em, comfortin' 'em. Whoever speaks in another language just builds up himself, but whoever prophesies builds up the whole church.

web_text: Now I desire to have you all speak with other languages, but rather that you would prophesy. For he is greater who prophesies than he who speaks with other languages, unless he interprets, that the assembly may be built up. But now, brothers, if I come to you speaking with other languages, what would I profit you, unless I speak to you either by way of revelation, or of knowledge, or of prophesying, or of teaching? Even things without life, giving a voice, whether pipe or harp, if they didn’t give a distinction in the sounds, how would it be known what is piped or harped? For if the trumpet gave an uncertain sound, who would prepare himself for war?
slang_text: I'd honestly be stoked if you all spoke in other languages, but I'd rather you all prophesy, fer sure. Whoever prophesies is bigger than whoever speaks in another language, unless he interprets it so the church actually gets built up. But now, brothers, if I show up talkin' in other languages, what good would that do you unless I actually give you revelation, or knowledge, or prophecy, or teaching? Even lifeless stuff that makes noise, like a flute or a harp -- if it didn't make distinct notes, how would anybody know what song's even playin'? And if a trumpet blasted out some garbled, uncertain sound, who's gonna gear up for battle off that?

web_text: So also you, unless you uttered by the tongue words easy to understand, how would it be known what is spoken? For you would be speaking into the air. There are, it may be, so many kinds of sounds in the world, and none of them is without meaning. If then I don’t know the meaning of the sound, I would be to him who speaks a foreigner, and he who speaks would be a foreigner to me. So also you, since you are zealous for spiritual gifts, seek that you may abound to the building up of the assembly.
slang_text: Same deal for you -- unless you say words that are actually easy to understand, how's anybody gonna know what you're even sayin'? You'd basically just be talkin' to thin air, no lie. There's a ton of different sounds and languages out there in the world, and every single one of 'em means somethin'. But if I don't know what a sound means, I'm a total stranger to the guy talkin', and he's a stranger to me. Same goes for you -- since you're all fired up about spiritual gifts, go after the ones that'll build up the church the most.

web_text: Therefore let him who speaks in another language pray that he may interpret. For if I pray in another language, my spirit prays, but my understanding is unfruitful. What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also. I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also. Otherwise if you bless with the spirit, how will he who fills the place of the unlearned say the “Amen” at your giving of thanks, seeing he doesn’t know what you say?
slang_text: So whoever speaks in another language oughta pray that he can also interpret it. 'Cause if I pray in another language, my spirit's prayin', sure, but my mind ain't gettin' anything outta it. So what's the move then? I'm gonna pray with my spirit, and I'm gonna pray with my mind too. I'm gonna sing with my spirit, and I'm gonna sing with my mind too. Otherwise, if you just bless God with your spirit, how's the regular dude in the back gonna say "Amen" to your thanks, if he's got no clue what you just said?

web_text: For you most certainly give thanks well, but the other person is not built up. I thank my God, I speak with other languages more than you all. However in the assembly I would rather speak five words with my understanding, that I might instruct others also, than ten thousand words in another language. Brothers, don’t be children in thoughts, yet in malice be babies, but in thoughts be mature.
slang_text: No doubt you're givin' thanks just fine, but the other dude ain't gettin' built up from it. I thank my God I speak in other languages more than all of you put together, fer sure. But in church, I'd way rather say five words that people actually understand, so I can teach somebody somethin', than ten thousand words in some other language nobody gets. Brothers, don't be kids in your thinkin' -- stay babies when it comes to bein' malicious, sure, but in your thinkin', grow up, be mature.

web_text: In the law it is written, “By men of strange languages and by the lips of strangers I will speak to this people. Not even thus will they hear me, says the Lord.” Therefore other languages are for a sign, not to those who believe, but to the unbelieving; but prophesying is for a sign, not to the unbelieving, but to those who believe. If therefore the whole assembly is assembled together and all speak with other languages, and unlearned or unbelieving people come in, won’t they say that you are crazy? But if all prophesy, and someone unbelieving or unlearned comes in, he is reproved by all, and he is judged by all.
slang_text: It's written right there in the law, "Through strange-language guys and stranger's lips I'll speak to this people, and even then they still won't listen to me, says the Lord." So other languages are a sign, not for the believers, but for the unbelievers; but prophecy's a sign for the believers, not the unbelievers. So if the whole church gets together and everybody's talkin' in other languages, and some regular unbelievin' dude walks in, won't he just think you're all trippin' and act like you're spazzin' out? But if everybody prophesies, and some unbeliever walks in, he gets called out by everybody, and everybody's judgin' him -- in a good way, straight to his heart.

web_text: And thus the secrets of his heart are revealed. So he will fall down on his face and worship God, declaring that God is among you indeed. What is it then, brothers? When you come together, each one of you has a psalm, has a teaching, has a revelation, has another language, has an interpretation. Let all things be done to build each other up. If any man speaks in another language, let it be two, or at the most three, and in turn; and let one interpret. But if there is no interpreter, let him keep silent in the assembly, and let him speak to himself, and to God.
slang_text: And that's how the secrets in his heart get exposed. So he's gonna fall flat on his face and worship God, sayin' straight up that God's totally with you guys, for real. So what's the deal then, brothers? When you come together, each one of you's got somethin' -- a psalm, a teachin', a revelation, another language, an interpretation. Let everything be done to build each other up, no exceptions. If anybody speaks in another language, let it be two, or three tops, one at a time, and let somebody interpret. But if there's no interpreter around, let him zip it in church, and just talk to himself and to God instead.

web_text: Let the prophets speak, two or three, and let the others discern. But if a revelation is made to another sitting by, let the first keep silent. For you all can prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be exhorted. The spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets,
slang_text: Let the prophets speak, two or three of 'em, and let everybody else weigh in on it. But if somebody else sittin' there gets a revelation, let the first guy hush up. 'Cause you can all prophesy one at a time, so everybody learns somethin' and everybody gets encouraged. And check it -- the prophets' spirits actually listen to the prophets,

web_text: for God is not a God of confusion, but of peace, as in all the assemblies of the saints. Let the wives be quiet in the assemblies, for it has not been permitted for them to be talking except in submission, as the law also says, if they desire to learn anything. “Let them ask their own husbands at home, for it is shameful for a wife to be talking in the assembly.” What!? Was it from you that the word of God went out? Or did it come to you alone?
slang_text: 'cause God ain't a God of chaos, he's a God of peace, same as it is in all the churches of the saints. Let the wives keep quiet in church -- they ain't allowed to just be talkin' out unless it's in submission, same as the law says too, if they wanna learn somethin'. "Let 'em ask their own husbands about it at home, 'cause it's straight-up shameful for a wife to be talkin' in church." What, seriously? Did God's word come outta you guys? Or did it only reach you and nobody else?

web_text: If any man thinks himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him recognize the things which I write to you, that they are the commandment of the Lord. But if anyone is ignorant, let him be ignorant. Therefore, brothers, desire earnestly to prophesy, and don’t forbid speaking with other languages. Let all things be done decently and in order.
slang_text: If any guy thinks he's a prophet, or thinks he's spiritual, he better recognize that what I'm writin' to you is the Lord's own command, no doubt about it. But if somebody wants to stay clueless, let 'em stay clueless, that's on them. So, brothers, go after prophesyin' hard, and don't shut down speakin' in other languages either. Just let everything be done decently and in order, no chaos allowed.

web_text: Now I declare to you, brothers, the Good News which I preached to you, which also you received, in which you also stand, by which also you are saved, if you hold firmly the word which I preached to you—unless you believed in vain. For I delivered to you first of all that which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures, that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,
slang_text: Now let me lay this out for you, brothers -- the Good News I preached to you, that you received, that you're standin' on, that's savin' you right now, if you hold onto the word I preached, unless you believed it for nothin'. 'Cause the first thing I handed down to you was what I myself received: that Christ died for our sins, just like the Scriptures said; that he got buried; that he got raised up on the third day, just like the Scriptures said too,

web_text: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve. Then he appeared to over five hundred brothers at once, most of whom remain until now, but some have also fallen asleep. Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles, and last of all, as to the child born at the wrong time, he appeared to me also.
slang_text: and that he showed up to Cephas, then to the twelve. Then he showed up to over five hundred brothers all at the same time, most of whom are still around today, though some have passed on since. Then he showed up to James, then to all the apostles, and last of all, like some kid born way outta season, he even showed up to me.

web_text: For I am the least of the apostles, who is not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the assembly of God. But by the grace of God I am what I am. His grace which was given to me was not futile, but I worked more than all of them; yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me. Whether then it is I or they, so we preach, and so you believed. Now if Christ is preached, that he has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
slang_text: 'Cause I'm the least of all the apostles, not even worthy to be called an apostle, since I persecuted God's church. But by God's grace, I am what I am, no doubt. His grace that got poured on me wasn't wasted -- I worked harder than all of 'em put together, though it wasn't really me, it was God's grace workin' through me. So whether it's me or them, this is what we preach, and this is what you believed, fer sure. Now if Christ is preached as raised from the dead, how come some of you dudes are sayin' there's no resurrection of the dead at all?

web_text: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, neither has Christ been raised. If Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain, and your faith also is in vain. Yes, we are also found false witnesses of God, because we testified about God that he raised up Christ, whom he didn’t raise up, if it is so that the dead are not raised. For if the dead aren’t raised, neither has Christ been raised.
slang_text: But if there's no resurrection of the dead at all, then Christ hasn't been raised either, plain and simple. And if Christ hasn't been raised, our whole preachin' is for nothin', and your faith's for nothin' too. Worse than that, we end up bein' straight-up liars about God, 'cause we testified that God raised up Christ, when he didn't -- if it's really true that the dead don't get raised. 'Cause if the dead don't get raised, neither did Christ.

web_text: If Christ has not been raised, your faith is vain; you are still in your sins. Then they also who are fallen asleep in Christ have perished. If we have only hoped in Christ in this life, we are of all men most pitiable. But now Christ has been raised from the dead. He became the first fruits of those who are asleep.
slang_text: If Christ hasn't been raised, your faith's worth nothin', and you're still stuck in your sins. And that means everybody who's died trustin' in Christ is just flat-out gone, no comin' back. If all we've got is hope in Christ for this life right here and nothin' more, we're the most pitiful people on the whole planet, way harsh. But here's the truth -- Christ HAS been raised from the dead. He's the first fruits of everybody who's fallen asleep.

web_text: For since death came by man, the resurrection of the dead also came by man. For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive. But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, then those who are Christ’s, at his coming. Then the end comes, when he will deliver up the Kingdom to God, even the Father; when he will have abolished all rule and all authority and power.
slang_text: 'Cause since death came through a man, the resurrection of the dead came through a man too. Just like everybody dies in Adam, so everybody gets made alive in Christ. But everybody in their own order -- Christ first, the first fruits, then everybody who's Christ's, when he comes back. Then comes the end, when he hands the whole Kingdom over to God the Father, after he's totally wiped out every rule, every authority, every power that stands against him.

web_text: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet. The last enemy that will be abolished is death. For, “He put all things in subjection under his feet.” But when he says, “All things are put in subjection”, it is evident that he is excepted who subjected all things to him. When all things have been subjected to him, then the Son will also himself be subjected to him who subjected all things to him, that God may be all in all.
slang_text: 'Cause he's gotta reign until he's put every single enemy under his feet. And the last enemy that gets wiped out is death itself. 'Cause it's written, "He put everything under subjection beneath his feet." But when it says "everything's subjected," obviously that doesn't include the one who subjected everything to him in the first place. So once everything's been put under him, then the Son himself will get put under the one who put everything under him, so that God can be everything to everybody, fer sure.

web_text: Or else what will they do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead aren’t raised at all, why then are they baptized for the dead? Why do we also stand in jeopardy every hour? I affirm, by the boasting in you which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily. If I fought with animals at Ephesus for human purposes, what does it profit me? If the dead are not raised, then “let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
slang_text: Or else what's the deal with folks gettin' baptized for the dead? If the dead don't get raised at all, why bother gettin' baptized for 'em in the first place? And why do we keep riskin' our necks every single hour? I'm tellin' you straight -- by the pride I've got in you through Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily, no lie. If I fought wild animals at Ephesus just for some human payoff, what's that even gettin' me? If the dead don't get raised, then "let's just eat, drink, and party, 'cause tomorrow we're dead anyway."

web_text: Don’t be deceived! “Evil companionships corrupt good morals.” Wake up righteously, and don’t sin, for some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame. But someone will say, “How are the dead raised?” and, “With what kind of body do they come?” You foolish one, that which you yourself sow is not made alive unless it dies.
slang_text: Don't get fooled! "Grody company corrupts good morals," fer sure. Wake up and get righteous, quit sinnin' -- some of you dudes flat-out don't know God, and that's on you, no lie, I'm sayin' this to shame you a little. But somebody's gonna ask, "How do the dead even get raised?" and "What kind of body do they show up with?" You total dipstick -- whatever you plant yourself doesn't even come to life unless it dies first.

web_text: That which you sow, you don’t sow the body that will be, but a bare grain, maybe of wheat, or of some other kind. But God gives it a body even as it pleased him, and to each seed a body of its own. All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one flesh of men, another flesh of animals, another of fish, and another of birds. There are also celestial bodies, and terrestrial bodies; but the glory of the celestial differs from that of the terrestrial.
slang_text: Whatever you plant, you're not plantin' the actual body it's gonna become -- you're just plantin' a bare seed, maybe wheat, maybe somethin' else. But God gives it a body exactly how he wants, and to each seed its own body, fer sure. Not all flesh is the same flesh either -- there's one flesh for people, another for animals, another for fish, another for birds. There's heavenly bodies and earthly bodies too, but the glory of the heavenly ones is totally different from the glory of the earthly ones.

web_text: There is one glory of the sun, another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for one star differs from another star in glory. So also is the resurrection of the dead. The body is sown perishable; it is raised imperishable. It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power. It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body and there is also a spiritual body.
slang_text: There's the sun's glory, the moon's glory, and the stars' glory -- and even star to star, the glory's totally different. Same deal with the resurrection of the dead. The body gets planted perishable, it gets raised up imperishable. It gets planted in dishonor, it gets raised in glory. It gets planted weak, it gets raised in power. It gets planted a natural body, it gets raised a spiritual body. There's a natural body, fer sure, but there's also a spiritual body.

web_text:
So also it is written, “The first man, Adam, became a living soul.”
 The last Adam became a life-giving spirit. However that which is spiritual isn’t first, but that which is natural, then that which is spiritual. The first man is of the earth, made of dust. The second man is the Lord from heaven. As is the one made of dust, such are those who are also made of dust; and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.
slang_text:
So it's written, "The first man, Adam, became a livin' soul."
The last Adam became a life-givin' spirit. But the spiritual doesn't come first, the natural comes first, then the spiritual. The first man's from the earth, made outta dust. The second man's the Lord from heaven. Just like the dusty guy, so are all of us dusty folks; and just like the heavenly guy, so are all of us who are heavenly too.

web_text: As we have borne the image of those made of dust, let’s also bear the image of the heavenly. Now I say this, brothers, that flesh and blood can’t inherit God’s Kingdom; neither does the perishable inherit imperishable. Behold, I tell you a mystery. We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed, in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.
slang_text: Just like we've worn the image of the dusty guy, let's wear the image of the heavenly guy too. Now here's what I'm tellin' you, brothers -- flesh and blood can't inherit God's Kingdom, and the perishable can't inherit what's imperishable, no way. Check it out, I'm about to lay a mystery on you: we're not all gonna sleep, but we're all gonna get changed, in a split second, in the blink of an eye, at the very last trumpet blast. 'Cause that trumpet's gonna sound, and the dead are gonna get raised up incorruptible, and we're all gonna get changed right along with 'em.

web_text:
For this perishable body must become imperishable, and this mortal must put on immortality. But when this perishable body will have become imperishable, and this mortal will have put on immortality, then what is written will happen: “Death is swallowed up in victory.” “Death, where is your sting?
Hades, where is your victory?” The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
slang_text:
'Cause this perishable body's gotta become imperishable, and this mortal body's gotta put on immortality. And when this perishable body's finally become imperishable, and this mortal's finally put on immortality, then what's written is gonna happen for real: "Death's totally swallowed up in victory."
"Death, where's your sting now?
Hades, where's your victory now?" The sting of death is sin, and the power behind sin is the law.

web_text: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ. Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the Lord’s work, because you know that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.
slang_text: But thank God, for real, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ. So, my beloved brothers, stand firm, don't budge an inch, always go all in on the Lord's work, 'cause you know your hard work ain't for nothin' in the Lord. Not even close.

web_text: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I commanded the assemblies of Galatia, you do likewise. On the first day of the week, let each one of you save, as he may prosper, that no collections be made when I come. When I arrive, I will send whoever you approve with letters to carry your gracious gift to Jerusalem. If it is appropriate for me to go also, they will go with me.
slang_text: Now about the collection for the saints -- same deal I told the churches in Galatia to do, you guys do it too. On the first day of the week, everybody set somethin' aside, however much you're doin' good financially, so there's no scramblin' to collect stuff when I show up. When I get there, I'll send whoever you approve of, with letters, to carry your generous gift to Jerusalem. And if it makes sense for me to go too, they can just roll with me.

web_text: But I will come to you when I have passed through Macedonia, for I am passing through Macedonia. But with you it may be that I will stay, or even winter, that you may send me on my journey wherever I go. For I do not wish to see you now in passing, but I hope to stay a while with you, if the Lord permits. But I will stay at Ephesus until Pentecost,
slang_text: I'm gonna come see you once I've passed through Macedonia, 'cause that's where I'm headed right now. Might even hang out with you guys, maybe even winter there, so you can send me off wherever I gotta go next. I don't just wanna see you real quick passin' through -- I'm hopin' to stick around a while, if the Lord's cool with it. But for now, I'm stayin' put in Ephesus till Pentecost,

web_text: for a great and effective door has opened to me, and there are many adversaries. Now if Timothy comes, see that he is with you without fear, for he does the work of the Lord, as I also do. Therefore let no one despise him. But set him forward on his journey in peace, that he may come to me; for I expect him with the brothers. Now concerning Apollos, the brother, I strongly urged him to come to you with the brothers; and it was not at all his desire to come now; but he will come when he has an opportunity.
slang_text: 'cause a huge, wide-open door's swung open for me here, though there's plenty of adversaries too, no lie. Now if Timothy shows up, make sure he ain't gotta be scared around you guys, 'cause he's doin' the Lord's work same as I am. So don't let anybody dis him. Send him on his way in peace so he can come back to me, 'cause I'm expectin' him with the brothers. Now about Apollos, our brother -- I straight-up urged him hard to come to you with the brothers, but he really didn't wanna come right now; he'll come when he gets the chance.

web_text: Watch! Stand firm in the faith! Be courageous! Be strong! Let all that you do be done in love. Now I beg you, brothers (you know the house of Stephanas, that it is the first fruits of Achaia, and that they have set themselves to serve the saints), that you also be in subjection to such, and to everyone who helps in the work and labors.
slang_text: Stay alert! Stand firm in the faith! Be brave! Be strong! Let everything you do get done in love, no exceptions. Now I'm beggin' you guys, brothers -- y'all know Stephanas's house, the first fruits of Achaia, and how they've set themselves up to serve the saints -- so submit yourselves to folks like that, and to anybody else out there who's puttin' in the work and grindin' hard.

web_text: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus; for that which was lacking on your part, they supplied. For they refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore acknowledge those who are like that. The assemblies of Asia greet you. Aquila and Priscilla greet you much in the Lord, together with the assembly that is in their house. All the brothers greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
slang_text: I'm totally stoked that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus showed up -- they filled in whatever was missin' from you guys, no lie. They refreshed my spirit and yours both. So give props to dudes like that. The churches of Asia say hey. Aquila and Priscilla say hey big time in the Lord, along with the church that meets at their house. All the brothers say hey. Greet each other with a holy kiss.

web_text:
This greeting is by me, Paul, with my own hand. If any man doesn’t love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be accursed.
 Come, Lord! The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you. My love to all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.
slang_text:
This part right here I'm writin' with my own hand, Paul here. If any dude out there doesn't love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be cursed, way harsh, but that's how it is.
Come on, Lord! The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. My love to every single one of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.
